section: # -- client_household.csv: Household, economic assumptions, payroll, wellness, and Social Security.

label: member_1_nickname
notes: Short name used in all reports and charts. Leave blank to use the first name.

label: member_1_dob
notes: M/D/YYYY

label: member_1_retirement_date
notes: Year parsed from date; base assumption

label: member_1_mortality_age
notes: Plan horizon = DOB year + this age

label: member_2_nickname
notes: Short name used in all reports and charts. Leave blank to use the first name.

label: member_2_dob
notes: M/D/YYYY

label: residence_state
notes: State tax and residency analysis

label: filing_status
notes: MFJ -> Single in year after first death

label: survivor_filing_status
notes: Filing status after first death: Single | HOH

label: survivor_has_dependent
notes: Enables Qualifying Surviving Spouse brackets for two years after first death when applicable.

label: inflation_general
notes: CPI for spending, home, autos

label: social_security_cola
notes: SS benefit COLA

label: medicare_part_b_inflation
notes: Part B premium growth

label: medicare_part_d_inflation
notes: Part D premium growth

label: portfolio_nominal_return
notes: Gross return before expense drag

label: fed_tax_bracket_inflator
notes: Annual bracket inflation

label: social_security_taxable_fraction
notes: Fraction of SS subject to income tax

label: annuity_default_dividend_rate
notes: Default dividend rate applied ONLY to annuity income streams that have no explicit dividend rate entered of their own (Income Streams > dividend_rate). Editing this has no effect on any annuity stream that already carries its own rate.

label: annuity_default_additional_income_pct
notes: Reinvested into base

label: annuity_default_pay_in_cash_pct
notes: Distributed as cash income

label: reinvest_dividends_default
notes: Global switch: reinvest every investment account's dividends/interest into the same holding instead of letting them convert to cash inside the account. When YES, this applies to every investment account and the per-account overrides below are ignored.

label: cash_yield_rate
notes: Growth rate applied to dividends/interest that convert to cash inside an account (Reinvest Dividends = NO) instead of compounding with the rest of the holding. That cash stays in the account and keeps earning this rate indefinitely.

label: annual_premium_base_year
notes: Annual Pre-65 Healthcare Premium per person before Medicare, such as ACA marketplace, COBRA, or retiree bridge coverage. The model spends this until Medicare starts and inflates it with medical inflation.

label: part_b_base_premium_monthly
notes: Monthly Medicare Part B base premium for one Medicare-enrolled person before IRMAA surcharges.

label: part_d_base_premium_monthly
notes: Monthly Medicare Part D prescription-drug premium for one Medicare-enrolled person before IRMAA surcharges.

label: part_g_base_premium_monthly
notes: Monthly Medicare Supplement Plan G / Medigap-style premium for one Medicare-enrolled person. Enter $0 if no supplement is modeled.

label: annual_oop_estimate_today
notes: Annual household medical OOP cap for deductibles, copays, dental, vision, prescriptions, and uncovered care.

label: survivor_pct_of_higher_benefit
notes: 100 = full higher benefit continues to survivor

label: survivor_benefit_uses_deceased_claim_age
notes: Survivor benefit uses the deceased worker claim-age factor.

label: oop_utilization_pct
notes: Share of the annual household medical OOP cap to model as non-premium medical spending when detailed medical expense rows are not provided.

label: monthly_pia_at_fra_today_dollars
notes: Optional. If blank, the engine back-solves approximate PIA from the age-70 amount.

label: monthly_pia_at_fra_today_dollars
notes: Optional. If blank, the engine back-solves approximate PIA from the age-70 amount.

label: claim_age
notes: Modeled per spouse using early/delayed retirement factors.

label: claim_age
notes: Modeled per spouse using early/delayed retirement factors.

label: spousal_benefits_enabled
notes: Approximate spousal top-up from the higher PIA when beneficial.

label: enabled
notes: Model ACA premium tax credits for pre-65 bridge years.

label: household_size
notes: Household size for federal poverty level calculation.

label: federal_poverty_level_base_year
notes: Base-year FPL for ACA premium tax credit model.

label: benchmark_silver_premium_annual
notes: Annual benchmark silver premium before subsidy.

label: applicable_pct_cap
notes: Maximum household contribution percentage under enhanced subsidy rules.

label: enhanced_subsidies_through_year
notes: Last year enhanced ACA subsidy schedule applies.

label: target_state
notes: Target relocation state abbreviation (e.g. FL TX NV) for the residency comparison workbook sheet

label: illinois_baseline_annual
notes: Current-state (Illinois) homeowners insurance annual premium

label: target_state_annual
notes: Target-state homeowners insurance annual premium

label: notes
notes: Homeowners insurance comparison notes

label: illinois_baseline_annual
notes: Current-state (Illinois) auto insurance annual premium

label: target_state_annual
notes: Target-state auto insurance annual premium

label: notes
notes: Auto insurance comparison notes
